title: Model Routing as a Cost-Reduction Primitive

questions.question: What is the core idea of model cascading?
options: Run two models in parallel and average the outputs
Run every request on the most expensive model first
Run a cheap model first, escalate to a frontier model only on low confidence or refusal

questions.question: Which four signals does the lesson list for routing decisions?
options: Task classification, prompt length, embedding similarity to known-hard set, and self-confidence from a first-pass
GPU temperature, fan speed, room humidity, time of day

questions.question: What is the expected latency profile of a cascade router?
options: About 1.2x median latency (cheap run plus verify), about 2x on escalated requests (~10% of traffic)

questions.question: Which routing pattern adds 5-10ms latency up front and is fastest overall?
What is cheap-model drift in routing?
options: The cheap model becomes more expensive
Cascade falls through to frontier 100% of the time
Task distribution shifts but the trained router keeps sending requests to the cheap model, silently degrading quality
A latency drift in the cheap model

questions.question: Which guard does the lesson recommend to catch routing drift?
options: Online quality metrics — thumbs-up/down per route, LLM-judge on held-out samples, escalation rate, refusal rate
